This brought them into contact with the crew of the ''Ghost'' and Ahsoka, who soundly [[Duel in Hammertown|defeated]] them in combat.{{Rebels|The Future of the Force}} Later, the Seventh Sister and the Fifth Brother took part in an [[Battle of Garel|unsuccessful attempt]] to capture the rebels in [[Garel]] [[Garel City|City]].{{Rebels|Legacy (episode)}} Three years before the [[Battle of Yavin]],''[[Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know, Updated and Expanded]]'' states that [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] was 16 years old during her [[Mission on Lothal|mission]] on [[Lothal]]. Since ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' establishes that Organa was [[Reproduction|born]] in [[19 BBY]], the mission must take place in [[3 BBY]]. ''[[Dawn of Rebellion]]'' dates the [[Attack on Phoenix Squadron (3 BBY)|attack]] on [[Phoenix Squadron]] to three years before the [[Battle of Yavin]], or [[3 BBY]] according to ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]''{{'}} [[Galactic standard calendar|dating system]]. As this duel takes place between these two events, it must also take place in 3 BBY. the Fifth Brother and the Seventh Sister spent several [[Standard month|months]] pursuing Jarrus and Bridger. On one occasion, they managed to track the Jedi to the planet [[Oosalon]] where they [[Duel on Oosalon|fought]] with them. Despite their efforts, the Jedi managed to escape and regroup with the Spectres. Later, the Inquisitors followed Jarrus, Bridger, and Tano to the [[Lothal Jedi Temple]]. After forcing their way into the [[Jedi temple|temple]], the two operatives were attacked by [[Force vision|ghostly apparitions]] of the Jedi Temple Guards. One of these apparitions was the old Grand Inquisitor. The Fifth Brother and Seventh Sister survived the encounter and reported to Lord Vader, who vowed to put an end to the Jedi threat. ===Crossover at Malachor=== {{Quote|You're the fourth Inquisitor we've seen. How many are there?''"
"''More than enough for the two of you. Nothing can save you.|Kanan Jarrus and the Eighth Brother|}} Sometime after the events of the Lothal Jedi Temple, Kanan, Ezra, and Ahsoka [[Mission to Malachor|traveled]] to [[Malachor]], following a hint given by [[Grand Master]] [[Yoda]]. When they arrived there, the [[Eighth Brother]], who was already there hunting an individual known as "the shadow", attacked them. Soon after, Bridger became separated from the others and encountered the castaway Sith Lord Maul. As this was happening, Tano and Jarrus chased and captured the Eighth Brother, who unbeknownst to them sent a distress signal to his fellow Inquisitors, the Fifth Brother and the Seventh Sister. Maul and Bridger together entered the [[Malachor Sith Temple|Sith temple]] on Malachor and retrieved [[Sith holocron (Malachor)|the]] [[Sith holocron]] inside. [[File:The_light_and_the_dark.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The three Inquisitors duel Ahsoka Tano, Kanan Jarrus, and the dark side warrior, Maul, on Malachor]] Shortly later, they found the Fifth Brother, Seventh Sister, and Eighth Brother dueling Tano and Jarrus. Maul and Bridger intervened and the Inquisitors were forced to retreat. While attempting to plant the Sith [[holocron]] in the [[Sith temple]]'s obelisk, they encountered the Imperial operatives yet again. Maul began [[Force choke|Force choking]] the Seventh Sister and urged Bridger to strike her down. When Bridger could not bring himself to do it, Maul then threw [[Maul's lightsaber|his lightsaber]] at her, killing her instantly. The duo then found Tano and Jarrus once more fighting the Fifth and Eighth Brothers. After the [[Togruta]] destroyed the [[Fifth Brother's lightsaber]], the [[Zabrak]] warrior made quick work of him with a fatal slash to the gut. During this time, Jarrus had also managed to slice the [[Eighth Brother's lightsaber]]. Cornered and outnumbered, the Inquisitor attempted to escape by using his lightsaber to hover away but the lightsaber malfunctioned in flight and sent the Eighth Brother plummeting to his death at the base of the temple below. ===Disappearance=== {{Quote|During the early years of the Empire, the red blades of the Inquisitorius cut a swath through the galaxy as they wiped any remaining traces of the Jedi Order from existence. Once the Jedi were finally considered extinct by the Empire, the Inquisitorius vanished into the darkness as well.|Luke Skywalker|''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''}} [[File:LinaGrafAsFourthSister-ReturnToVadersCastle4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Lina Graf impersonating the Fourth Sister.]] In [[2 BBY]],''[[Dawn of Rebellion]]'' dates the events of ''Star Wars Rebels: Steps Into Shadow'' seventeen years after the end of the [[Clone Wars]], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[19 BBY]]; therefore, the events of ''Steps Into Shadow'' must take place in [[2 BBY]]. around six months after{{SW|url=episodes/steps-into-shadow-trivia-gallery|text="Steps Into Shadow" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 6}} the incident on Malachor, Governor Tarkin agreed to have [[Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo|Thrawn]] finish what Lord Vader and his Inquisitors had started. The [[Chiss]] pursued the rebels until his defeat at the [[Liberation of Lothal]].{{Rebels|Family Reunion – and Farewell}} Another Inquisitor was the [[Third Brother]], whose actions and fate were mysteries to those unfamiliar with him, with the same being true for those unfamiliar with the Third Sister. The Fourth Sister continue to serve the Inquisitorius. She found Offee and stabbed her, but found herself lost in a cave. With the help of Offee, she navigated out of the cave while carrying Offee's body. Encouraged by Offee to leave the Inquisitorius, the Fourth Sister did so, leaving her [[Fourth Sister's hilt|lightsaber]] in the cave.{{TOTE|The Way Out}} Her armor would fall into the hands of rebel Commander [[Lina Graf]], who employed it to impersonate her on at least one occasion. By [[1 BBY]],{{FilmDate|IV|1}} the Jedi Order was largely thought to be extinct by the Empire.{{Film|IV}} Once their objective of wiping out these Force-wielders was completed, the Inquisitors themselves vanished as well. At a meeting aboard the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]], Governor Tarkin expressed his belief that Lord Vader was the last practitioner of what he termed their religion after believing there was no way Kenobi was still alive. By this time, the Emperor deemed the Inquisitorius' purpose obsolete as they outlived their usefulness.''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Sith]]'' The reasons for the disappearance of the Inquisitorius was relatively unclear to the greater galaxy. It was speculated by historian [[Beaumont Kin]] that the lack of viable Force users to draw from meant that the Inquisitors just eventually died out. However, Kin thought this fate unlikely, instead highlighting Palpatine's own words in referring to the group as "obsolete" which brought forth the possibility that Palpatine simply killed them off partly due to the lack of viable Jedi targets by that point, as well as Vader likely seeing how the Inquisitors could threaten his personal plans, thus pushing Vader to swiftly deal with such threats in haste.''[[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]'' An Inquisitor named [[Marrok]], however, would be active after the Empire's fall but joined his fallen brother and sisters when he was defeated by Tano.{{Databank|marrok-inquisitor|Marrok (Inquisitor)}} ===Legacy=== ====The Grand Inquisitor's damnation==== {{Quote|I have served… for so long. Will there never… be a chance… of release?''"
"''You are but a tool, shaped to serve my purposes. You will… continue.|The Inquisitor's spirit and Darth Vader|}} Although the Inquisitorius disappeared from the galaxy, its actions were still felt for [[Standard decade|decades]] to come. Verla, who escaped the Inquisitors' attack on Mon Cala, came to fear the dark siders and fled into exile on the planet [[Serelia]], where she became a [[Fishing|fisherwoman]]. When she was sought out by Lord Vader’s son aspiring Jedi [[Luke Skywalker]] in [[3 ABY]],The events of ''Star Wars'' 5 take place in [[3 ABY]] according to ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''. she initially ran from the boy, believing him to be a pawn of the Inquisitorius. Later, she explained to Skywalker that Vader and his minions had hunted down and destroyed the Jedi in the Great Purge, leaving her to believe that anyone who aspired to be a Jedi was nothing more than a fool. Despite her warnings, however, Skywalker insisted on continuing the Jedi path, and on Verla's instruction traveled to [[Tempes]] in the Outer Rim, searching to replace the [[Skywalker lightsaber|lightsaber]] he had lost in a [[Duel on Cloud City|duel]] with Vader.[[Star Wars (2020) 6|''Star Wars'' (2020) 6]] [[File:GrandInquisitorGhost-2020StarWars6.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The spirit of the Grand Inquisitor resided in an ancient Jedi outpost in Tempes.]] On Tempes, the trainee discovered a horrifying secret: the spirit of the Grand Inquisitor haunted an [[Jedi outpost (Tempes)|ancient Jedi outpost]]. Despite his death above Mustafar, the Inquisitor's indentured servitude to the Sith did not end with death. Lord Vader prevented the Grand Inquisitor's spirit from disappearing and bound him to reside on Tempes, where he sat for years to protect the outpost from people seeking the [[Jedi artifact]]s. The spirit battled the Jedi. The ensuing duel ended when Skywalker disarmed the Grand Inquisitor by cutting off his hand and hitting him in the chest, making the spirit disappear. Sometime after Skywalker's departure, Vader, who felt the confrontation between Skywalker and the Inquisitor through the Force, arrived at the outpost. The spirit confessed the boy was too strong and tried to convince Vader to free him from his curse. The Sith Lord, however, was dissatisfied the Inquisitor let the young man escape and ordered his underling to continue. As for Skywalker, he eventually went on to destroy the Sith and reforge the Jedi Order when he saved his father.{{Film|VIII}} ====Surviving remnants==== [[File:Marrok-TDS2025.png|thumb|right|250px|Marrok survived the Galactic Empire and became a mercenary.]] Despite the Inquisitorius being disbanded during the reign of the Empire, Marrok was active in the era of the [[New Republic]]. Marrok became a [[mercenary]] for [[Morgan Elsbeth]] and came into conflict with Tano during the [[Quest to find Ezra Bridger|search]] for Bridger, dueling her during a [[Mission to Corellia (New Republic)|mission to Corellia]]{{Ahsoka|Part Two: Toil and Trouble}} and later engaging her from the [[cockpit]] of his [[Marrok's Fiend fighter|Fiend fighter]] in a [[dogfight in the Denab system]].{{Ahsoka|Part Three: Time to Fly}} In the subsequent [[Incident on Seatos]], Marrok met his end at the hands of Tano, with her final lightsaber strike breaking through his armor and causing him to explode into a green mist.{{Ahsoka|Part Four: Fallen Jedi}} Shortly after, Elsbeth still had access to the [[Inquisitorial database]] and used it to follow through with Thrawn's order to know everything about Tano.{{Ahsoka|Part Seven: Dreams and Madness}} ====Late New Republic Era==== {{Quote|The Jedi aren't my story. So what if I'm meant to become one of these Inquisitors, instead?''"
"''I believe we've learned that they no longer exist.''"
"''But what if they come back? What if my vision was of the future and I become some sort of anti-Jedi Knight. [...] What if the Force awakens and there are those who want to snuff it out again? What if there are dark side users who are meant to do such a thing, and I'm meant to become one of them?|Karr Nuq Sin and [[RZ-7]]|''[[Force Collector]]''}} [[File:Tony-foti-starwars-forcecollector-fullwrap-art.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The legacy of the Inquisitorius was confronted by the Force collector Karr Nuq Sin (pictured)]] In [[32 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: Timelines]] dates the events of ''[[Force Collector]]'' to [[32 ABY]]. the Inquisitors and their history, much like the Jedi they hunted, was little-known. By this time the antiques [[collector]] [[Dok-Ondar]] acquired a broken lightsaber that once belonged to the Grand Inquisitor. When [[Karr Nuq Sin]], a Force-sensitive teenager seeking to understand his connection with the Force and the ways of the Jedi, visited [[Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities]] hoping to find Jedi artifacts, Dok-Ondar showed him the weapon. He explained the mission of the Inquisitors to Nuq Sin and the boy had never heard of them or the fact that the Jedi were hunted down after the Clone Wars. When Nuq Sin, who possessed the [[Force power]] of [[psychometry]], touched the two broken pieces of the lightsaber he experienced two visions simultaneously: one of his former Jedi Padawan great-grandfather [[Naq Med]] fighting the Grand Inquisitor and another of the Grand Inquisitor fighting and killing a different Jedi. The overlapping visions gave Nuq Sin, who strongly resembled his relative, the impression that he had witnessed his future self kill a Jedi. The visions caused him to doubt whether he was meant to become a Jedi and fear that in his future the defunct Inquisitorius would be resurrected and he would become one of its constituents. The [[First Order]], the government that rose from the ashes of the Empire following its fall after the [[New Republic]]'s [[Battle of Jakku|victory]] at the Battle of [[Jakku]] and the subsequent signing of the [[Galactic Concordance]], used interrogation chairs, based on those used by the Imperial Inquisition, to painfully [[Torture|extract]] information from their [[prisoner]]s.''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]'' Around the time of the [[First Order-Resistance War|war]] between the First Order and the [[Resistance]], Master Skywalker wrote a [[The Secrets of the Jedi|book]] chronicling what he knew of the history of the Jedi Order. In the book, Skywalker included a brief history of the Inquisitorius and their mission of hunting the Jedi, identified several of them by the names they bore as Inquisitors and noted that once the Jedi were considered extinct, the Inquisitors themselves vanished. He also wrote about the signature double-bladed spinning lightsabers used by the Imperial operatives. [[File:KnightsOfRen-Empire.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The sinister Knights of Ren would have been ideal Inquisitors had circumstances been different.]] The [[Knights of Ren]] were a band of dark side warriors who had once acted as independent agents away from the eye of the Sith during the [[Galactic Civil War]].[[Crimson Reign 1|''Crimson Reign'' 1]] To try and prove themselves to the Sith and not be destroyed, the Knights submitted Sidious and Vader in the aftermath of the [[Battle at Amaxine Station]][[Hidden Empire 5|''Hidden Empire'' 5]] and served the resurrected Sidious in secret after his death at the [[Battle of Endor]]. Keeping their allegiance to Sidious secret,''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition]]'' they would do the bidding of Vader's grandson [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] during the [[New Republic Era|age of the New Republic]]. They would have been prime candidates to become Inquisitors if they could have been tamed.''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' Historian [[Beaumont Kin]] [[Exegol Excavation Project|researched]] and and recounted details about Inquisitorius in a dedicated chapter in the ''[[The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]];'' Kin recounted their roles as Jedi hunters and assassins, their efforts to de-facing the Jedi Order, and their role in the Imperial army. He also spoke the extent of their relationship with Darth Vader, with their presence being more public compared to their master and the fear he illicit, citing a battle between former Jedi Knight [[Kanan Jarrus]] and the [[The Grand Inquisitor|Grand Inquisitor]] as evidence when the latter killed himself rather than face punishment for failure. Kin believed that the majority of Inquisitorius were likely killed by Sidious and Vader, supporting this with a passage from Sidious's [[The Secrets of the Sith|''The Secrets of the Sith'']] where he stated he deemed them "obsolete". Kin held several theories their obsolescence, citing a lack of Jedi targets from their success made them a drain on Imperial resources and those who threaten Vader's plans were seen as a liability.
